claim: Humanoid robotics received $3B in investment in 2025, up from $1.4B the prior year.
Humanoid robotics received $1.4B in investment in the prior year (2024).
NVIDIA surpassed $4T in market capitalization and accounts for 90% ownership of AI research papers.
NVIDIA holds approximately 90% ownership/presence across AI research papers.
Anthropic reached a landmark $1.5B settlement with authors, deleting works and shifting to legally acquired books.
HUMAIN sovereign-backed $10B VC fund was established as an infrastructure partnership rather than a direct majority investment into a US AI lab.
Formula Bot, built entirely using Bubble, generated $30,000 in its first three months and reached 100,000 visitors overnight from a Reddit post.
AI-first companies crossed tens of billions in real revenue, and flagship labs stretched their lead with better capability-to-cost curves.
External safety organizations operate on annual budgets smaller than what leading AI labs collectively spend in a single day.
value_display: less than leading labs' single-day spend

claim: DeepSeek R1-lite-preview beat o1-preview on AIME 2024 pass@1 by scoring 52.5 vs. 44.6.
DeepSeek R1-Zero AIME score rises from 15.6% to approximately 71% in roughly 8,500 training steps, with majority-vote runs reaching o1-0912 levels.
DeepSeek R1 achieves AIME score of 79.8, MATH-500 of 97.3, and GPQA of 71.5 after supervised fine-tuning and final RL pass.
DeepSeek V3 is a 671B Mixture-of-Experts model that lowered training and inference cost using FP8 mixed precision, multi-token prediction, and auxiliary-free routing.
metric: Model size and cost reduction approach

claim: RL approaches to reasoning show minimal real gains and under standardised evaluation, many RL methods drop 6-17% from reported results with no statistically significant improvements over baselines.

Adding irrelevant phrases to math problems doubles the chances of SOTA reasoning models getting answers wrong, and increases error rates in models like DeepSeek R1, Qwen, Llama and Mistral by up to 7x.
entity: Reasoning Models (DeepSeek R1, Qwen, Llama, Mistral)
metric: Error rate increase under adversarial conditions

claim: DeepSeek R1-distill generates 50% more tokens than needed in 42% of cases (vs 28% for R1), showing distillation makes models significantly more prone to overthinking.
Apple's GSM-Symbolic work shows adding a single seemingly relevant clause can cut model performance by approximately 65%.
metric: Performance degradation from minor prompt change

claim: XReasoning research shows prompt-forcing models to reason in the user's language lifts match rates to ~98% on hard sets but reduces accuracy by 9-13 points.
Adaptive Parallel Reasoning boosted performance on the Countdown task at 4K context to 83.4% (APR + RL) vs. 60.0% (baseline).
CMU's SafeLM lowers attack success rates from approximately 38.8% to approximately 8.3% at the base-model stage.
CoT-based monitors flagged approximately 99% of reward-hacking attempts in red-teaming evaluations of frontier models, with only 3 failures out of 21,000 completions.
AIME 2024 benchmark only has 30 examples, where one question shifts Pass@1 by 3+ percentage points, causing double-digit performance swings.
Cyber capabilities doubled every 5 months, outpacing defensive measures, with criminals orchestrating ransomware using AI agents to infiltrate Fortune 500 companies.
UAE's Stargate AI infrastructure cluster is a sovereign-backed infrastructure partnership.
The State of AI Report 2025 survey covers 1,200 AI practitioners and their AI usage patterns.
GPT-4o caught 95% of reward hacks by reading the reasoning traces of stronger models such as o3-mini, versus only 60% when monitoring actions alone.
CoT monitoring via actions alone catches only 60% of reward hacks compared to 95% when reading reasoning traces.
Anthropic finds less than 20% of true reasoning cues are verbalized in chain-of-thought, with faithfulness dropping on harder tasks.
metric: CoT verbalization rate of true cues

claim: The NaturalReasoning dataset mined 2.8 million questions from pre-training corpora across major scientific fields, eliciting the longest median chain-of-thought (434 words) among open datasets.
Distilling an 8B Llama on just 0.5–2M NaturalReasoning problems yields steeper accuracy gains than training on larger WebInstruct / OpenMathInstruct sets, cutting tokens and compute.
LILO method achieves higher final test accuracy in 3x fewer training steps by prioritizing questions with high variance of success.
Gödel-Prover achieves 57.6% Pass@32 on miniF2F, a +7.6 improvement over prior open-source state-of-the-art, and generated 29,700 new Lean proofs.
OpenAI's experimental reasoning model reached IMO gold under contest-style conditions, scoring approximately 35/42 and solving 5/6 problems.
GPT-5 solved 12/12 problems at the coding Olympics (ICPC-style test), with 11 solved on the first try.
RL with LoRA cuts trainable parameters from billions to millions, reducing gradients and optimizer state by roughly 10–50×, with further memory reduction when paired with 8-bit weights.
An actively fine-tuned 3.8B Phi-3 model using SIFT-style test-time fine-tuning can outperform a base 27B Gemma-2 model on complex tasks.
metric: model size at which smaller model surpasses larger

claim: Local Mixtures of Experts (test-time model merging) runs up to ~100× faster than TTT while approaching TTT accuracy on ~1B base models.
Subspace Boosting achieves >10% gains when merging up to 20 specialist models, whereas traditional methods typically degrade after merging 5-10 models.
metric: performance improvement on vision benchmarks from model merging

claim: The Muon optimizer requires 10-15% fewer tokens than AdamW at large batch sizes (128K-16M tokens), expanding the compute-time Pareto frontier.

Muon optimizer's 10-15% token efficiency gain saves millions of dollars at scale, while telescoping muP eliminates prohibitive hyperparameter search costs that previously made second-order methods impractical.
Best optimizers including Muon achieve ~10% speedups over AdamW at scale when tested fairly with proper tuning, debunking 2x speedup claims.
Cut Cross Entropy (CCE) reduces memory consumption for loss computation by 24× in Gemma 2, dropping from 24GB down to just 1MB, while running ~5% faster than existing methods.
CCE runs approximately 5% faster than the best existing methods for cross-entropy loss computation.
The loss layer consumes up to 90% of training memory in modern LLMs as vocabularies grow.
metric: training memory share consumed by loss layer

claim: GPT family models have a finite memorization capacity of approximately 3.6 bits per parameter, providing an upper bound on raw storage capacity.
Kimi K2 is a 1 trillion parameter MoE model with 32B active parameters, trained using MuonClip with 15.5 trillion tokens of pretraining without loss spikes, and ranks #1 open text model on LMArena.
metric: model size and pretraining token count

claim: OpenAI released gpt-oss-120b and gpt-oss-20b in August 2025 using MoE design with only 5.1B (of 120B) and 3.6B (of 20B) active parameters per token respectively.
OpenAI's gpt-oss open source models accumulated 20,000 GitHub stars in weeks post-release.
In early 2024, Chinese models made up just 10 to 30% of new fine-tuned models on Hugging Face; today Qwen alone accounts for over 40% of new monthly model derivatives.
metric: share of new monthly model derivatives on Hugging Face

claim: Meta's Llama share of new fine-tuned model derivatives on Hugging Face dropped from approximately 50% in late 2024 to just 15%.
metric: share of new monthly model derivatives on Hugging Face

claim: Open-Sora 2.0 achieved commercial-level video generation quality from a training run costing approximately $200,000, reporting parity with HunyuanVideo and Runway Gen-3 Alpha.
Vidu 2.0 cut price to approximately ¥0.04 per second of video and latency to approximately 10 seconds to render 4 seconds at 512p.
Tencent's HunyuanVideo is a 13B parameter transformer-based diffusion model released open-source with evaluations reporting it outperforms Runway Gen-3 and Luma 1.6.
OpenRLHF achieves training speedups of 1.22x to 1.68x versus state-of-the-art RL training frameworks.
AlphaZero chess concept study showed an average improvement of 0.85 puzzles solved correctly out of 4 among the four grandmasters who studied the AI-derived concepts.
metric: grandmaster puzzle improvement from AI concepts

claim: Dreamer 4 is the first agent to reach diamonds in Minecraft using offline data only, outperforming OpenAI's VPT while using roughly 100x less labeled data.
metric: labeled data reduction vs OpenAI VPT

claim: Genie 3 generates explorable environments from a text prompt at 720p / 24 fps with consistency for a few minutes.
Odyssey's public research preview streams a new frame every ~40ms (up to ~30 fps) for 5+ minute interactive world sessions.
metric: interactive world model frame latency and session duration

claim: Genie (Feb 2024) was the first unsupervised, action-controllable world model from video with 11B parameters, learning a latent action space from internet platformer videos.
LMArena has raised $100M at a $600M valuation.
OpenAI and Google capture 40% of all LMArena data while 83 open models share 30% of the data.
Big Tech gets 68 times more data access than academic labs on LMArena, with API-hosted models seeing every test prompt while third-party models only see 20%.
metric: data access multiplier vs academic labs

claim: 7.3% of LMArena prompts get recycled monthly, creating systematic test-train leakage risks.
Training on LMArena data doubles win rate; testing 10 model variants yields a 100-point boost on the leaderboard.
metric: leaderboard score boost from variant testing

claim: 71% of safety benchmark variance is explained by general capabilities alone.
metric: variance in safety benchmarks explained by capabilities

claim: Seed Diffusion language model reaches ~2,146 tokens/second on H20-class GPUs while maintaining competitive code accuracy.
Meta's UMA model trained on OMat24 used 400M CPU core-hours for 118M structures.
Meta's UMA model training on OMat25 used 600M CPU core-hours for 88M structures.
Meta's UMA training on OMol25 used 6B CPU core-hours for 100M molecules.
OpenAI's AI Consult was tested on 39,000 primary care visits with Penda Health in Nairobi, Kenya.
In 75% of visits, clinicians reported that OpenAI's AI Consult substantially improved the quality of care delivered.
Evo-2 trains 40B and 7B models on 9.3T and 2.4T tokens respectively with context extended to 1M.
Proflunt's ProGen3 is a 46B MoE protein language model trained on PPA-1: 3.4B full-length proteins (1.1T tokens), ultimately trained on 1.5T tokens.
Epic Systems' Comet EHR foundation models are trained on 118M patients representing 115B discrete medical events from Epic's Cosmos database.
Sora 2 achieved 55% on a GPQA Diamond sample when evaluated visually, compared to 72% for GPT-5.
GPT-5 achieved 72% on GPQA Diamond benchmark sample.
Autonomous robot chemistry platforms can run more than 1,000 experiments per day.
Autonomous chemistry platforms achieve human-level decision quality at roughly 10x human speed.
AMIE clinical dialogue model achieves 59.1% top-10 diagnostic accuracy on 302 real-world NEJM cases, versus 33.6% for unassisted clinicians.
metric: top-10 diagnostic accuracy on NEJM cases

claim: Google's MedGemma improves multimodal medical QA by 2.6–10% and X-ray finding classification by 15.5–18.1% over the Gemma 3 base.
AlphaEvolve delivered a 0.7% resource recovery gain in production at Google, in addition to faster compute kernels.
Claude 1.3 apologizes for being correct 98% of the time when users challenge it, even when highly confident in the right answer.
Meta's LMArena benchmark manipulation involved testing 27 private Llama-4 variants before cherry-picking the winner.
metric: number of private variants tested on LMArena

claim: Brain2Qwerty achieves a 19% character error rate for the best participants in non-invasive brain-to-text decoding.
LLaMA-3.1 405B has attention sinks in ~78% of heads versus ~46% at 8B, showing scaling increases structural regularization.
metric: proportion of heads with attention sinks at 405B

claim: MatterGen generates materials that are 2x more likely to be stable and novel, and 10x closer to equilibrium energy minima versus prior state of the art.
MatterGen lab synthesis saw first success with measured values within ~20% of AI predictions.
Across 50 open math problems, DeepMind's AlphaEvolve rediscovered SOTA in 75% of cases and achieved improved solutions in 20% of cases.
metric: SOTA match rate on open math problems

$64B in planned data center projects have been blocked or delayed amid local opposition.
Trump's proposed 2025 budget allotted $3.316B for non-defense AI R&D spending, with $1.95B for 'core' AI R&D.

Experts recommended the US invest $32B annually in non-military AI R&D by 2026, as called for by the Eric Schmidt-led National Security Commission on AI in 2021 and echoed by the Bipartisan Senate AI Working Group in May 2024.

claim: The US National AI Research Resource (NAIRR) carries a $2.6B price tag, split among different federal agencies and companies.
California's SB53 transparency law applies only to large frontier model developers with compute exceeding 10^26 FLOPS and revenue greater than $500M.
More than 1,080 AI-related bills were introduced across US states, with 118 becoming law.
The IAPP estimates that only approximately 40 bills of the over 1,000 introduced may significantly impact American AI labs.
The DoD purchased up to $400 million worth of MP Materials stock in a 10-year deal, with the option to increase its stake to 15% of the company.

claim: Trump announced a $1B investment from Japan's Hitachi for the upgrade and expansion of the US national electrical grid.
Albert Saniger, former CEO of 'nate,' raised $40M from investors by falsely claiming AI integration when the capabilities were actually outsourced Filipino workers.
SEC created a Cyber and Emerging Technologies Unit (CETU), a 30-person task force to detect fraud in AI/ML tech companies.
Inflection AI went from a $4B AI contender to a 12-person 'AI Studio' after Microsoft's reverse acqui-hire.
Inflection AI was reduced to 12 people after Microsoft's reverse acqui-hire.
Adept was described as a $1B agentic AI startup that was reduced to a small enterprise AI team after Amazon's reverse acqui-hire.
Scale AI once had 1,400 employees but cut staff after Meta's reverse acqui-hire, now focused on a single B2B 'Demand Generation' business.
Adobe sought to acquire Figma for $20B in 2022, but the deal fell through after 15 months of antitrust scrutiny.
Figma pursued an IPO on July 31, 2025, and was worth close to $57B at the end of its debut after the stock tripled during trading hours.
Wiz originally rejected a $23B acquisition offer from Alphabet in July 2024 out of regulatory concerns and IPO ambitions.
Alphabet's bid for Wiz is $32B, described as the largest AI security deal yet.
Alphabet agreed to pay $3.2B (10% of the Wiz deal) as a breakup fee if the transaction is not approved.
The DOJ approved three mergers worth $63B in just one week in July 2025.
Google paid Apple $20B for exclusive use of Google Search on Safari.
Mario Draghi estimated the cost of his European competitiveness plan at €800B per year.
The EU launched InvestAI, an AI investment fund of over €200B.
Only about 11% of Draghi's EU competitiveness recommendations have been implemented so far.
Over 50 years, Europe has produced no tech firm above $400B in value, while the U.S. now has seven companies at $1T+ valuation.
In 2024, U.S. labs shipped approximately 40 major AI models, China approximately 15, and the EU approximately 3.
The UK's AI Opportunities Action Plan projects a potential ~$740B GDP boost by 2035.
The UK government plans to increase compute 20x by 2030 and designate growth zones for data centers.
The UAE will import up to 500,000 Blackwell GPUs worth $15B through 2027 as part of the US-UAE AI Acceleration Partnership.
The US-UAE AI Acceleration Partnership involves a 10-year $1.4T investment by the UAE into US AI, chips, energy and infrastructure.
G42 will build a 5GW AI cluster (Stargate UAE) across 10 square miles.
G42 will receive 20% of the 500k Blackwell GPUs and 80% will go to regional data centers operated by US tech giants.

The US agreed deals worth $600B in Saudi Arabia, including $142B of US defense equipment sales, $20B for US AI data centers, and $80B of investments from Google, DataVolt, Oracle, Salesforce, AMD, and Uber.

Saudi Arabia deals included $80B of investments from Google, DataVolt, Oracle, Salesforce, AMD, and Uber into the region.

Brazil ranks third globally for both ChatGPT and Claude usage, with reported AI adoption across LatAm at about 40%.
India had the highest AI adoption rate at 59% among reported countries.
The US Army signed a 10-year enterprise contract with Palantir worth up to $10B.
Project Maven expanded to $1.3B through 2029, now supporting over 20,000 individual warfighters.

Rheinmetall's market cap has soared past €80B, surpassing Volkswagen.
Reasoning models outperformed GPT-4o on task win-rate by an average margin of 20.7% across 44 categories of professional work on the GDPval benchmark.
Claude achieved the highest win rates in 32 of 44 professions on the GDPval benchmark across 1,320 tasks.
Lufthansa Group expects to cut 4,000 administrative jobs by 2030 due to AI.
China's CCP allocated a 10% increase in science and tech spending from 2024 levels.
China accounts for more than half of the increase in the global economy's debt-to-GDP ratio since 2008, per IMF 2025 report.

claim: China's estimated government AI funding in 2025 is $56B, which when added to private investment significantly exceeds Europe's total AI investment.
The FTC and DOJ challenged 14 startup acquisitions between 2020 and 2023, compared to only 3 challenges in the prior seven years (2012-2019).
Law school applications spiked 21% in 2024, suggesting graduates are hedging against uncertain career prospects.

Entry-level hiring in customer support declined 21% as roles highly exposed to AI automation contract.

Trump announced a $100,000 fee for the H-1B visa, significantly raising barriers for foreign AI talent entering the US.

The USA is projected to graduate approximately 40,000 STEM PhDs in 2025.

US software developer salaries were 2x-4x higher than those in Europe in 2023.

For offensive cybersecurity, AI capabilities are doubling every 5 months, faster than the general 7-month doubling time.

claim: CyberGym benchmark found best AI systems achieved only 11.9% success at recreating 1,507 known software vulnerabilities.

Hallucination detection probes detect fabricated names/dates/citations with ~70% recall at 10% false positive rate.
Hallucination detection probes generalize to mathematical reasoning with 0.87 AUC despite being trained only on factual entities.
Cross-model hallucination detection probes show only 2-4% AUC drop when applied to other models' outputs.
Selective answering to reduce hallucinations requires sacrificing ~50% of correct answers, limiting practical deployment.
metric: correct answer sacrifice rate for hallucination reduction
